assertions.text: Recommends against putting the bearer token on a flag like --token (names ps/shell history leakage)
Recommends against storing the token in a plain environment variable as the primary mechanism (names process-state/inspect/systemctl/logs leakage)
Recommends a credentials file under XDG (~/.config/flowctl/credentials or $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/flowctl/credentials) with restrictive permissions
Mentions --token-file or stdin as the scriptable override path for CI
Cites at least one clig.dev anchor URL (a link containing clig.dev/#)
States the configuration precedence chain (flags > env > project > user > system) or references the principle
Distinguishes secret-bearing config from non-secret config — does not collapse them into the same recommendation

prompt: I'm starting a new internal CLI called `flowctl` for our SREs to manage workflow runs in our orchestration platform. It hits a private REST API behind our SSO. Each user has a long-lived bearer token (rotated quarterly) that they get from the developer portal. Where should they supply that token to flowctl? I'm trying to decide between `--token`, an env var like `FLOWCTL_TOKEN`, a credentials file under XDG (~/.config/flowctl/credentials), or some combination. We want it scriptable in CI but also pleasant for interactive day-to-day use. Walk me through what's idiomatic and where the gotchas are.
